title: matelea ochracea
intro: Matelea ochracea, also known as the "Orange Vine" or "Yellow Wood-vine," is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Matelea ochracea include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, Matelea ochracea is typically used in the form of a decoction or infusion, where the dried roots and stems are steeped in hot water to release their bioactive compounds.
